framework.name: Hermes Agent
framework.description: ReAct-loop agent with self-learning skill system — auto-extracts reusable SKILL.md from completed tasks. 70+ tools, 6 terminal backends (Local/Docker/SSH/Daytona/Modal/Singularity), 18+ LLM providers, 20+ messaging channels. Built by Nous Research in Python.

modules.name: AIAgent Core
modules.description: Synchronous agent loop (~12K LOC). Orchestrates: provider selection → prompt construction → tool dispatch → retry/fallback → compression → persistence. 4 API modes: chat_completions, codex_responses, anthropic_messages, auxiliary_client.

modules.name: Skill Extractor
modules.description: Auto-learning: after successful task completion, analyzes the execution path and saves as reusable SKILL.md. Zero human curation required. Compatible with agentskills.io standard.

modules.name: Tool Registry
modules.description: Central registry: tools self-register at import time via registry.register(). 70+ tools across 28 toolsets: terminal, file ops, web search, browser (Browserbase), code sandbox, subagent delegation, MCP client.

modules.name: Terminal Backends
modules.description: 6 execution backends: Local shell, Docker container, SSH remote, Daytona cloud, Modal serverless (sleep/wake to near-zero cost), Singularity container. Pluggable architecture.

modules.name: Memory System
modules.description: Three-layer: static files (MEMORY.md + USER.md, frozen at session start for prompt cache), SQLite FTS5 full-text search (auto-summarized after each session), Honcho user modeling (dialectical, understands motivations).

modules.name: Prompt Builder
modules.description: Tiered assembly: stable (cached) → context (session) → volatile (current turn). Anthropic cache breakpoints for prefix caching. Cache stability is 'sacred' — system prompt never changes mid-conversation.

modules.name: Context Compressor
modules.description: Lossy summarization of middle conversation turns when context exceeds thresholds. Preserves session lineage (parent/child across compressions).

modules.name: Provider Resolver
modules.description: Shared runtime resolver: maps (provider, model) → (api_mode, api_key, base_url). 18+ providers: OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Bedrock, DeepSeek, Gemini, GitHub Copilot, etc. OAuth + credential pools.

modules.name: Gateway Server
modules.description: Long-running process with ~20 platform adapters: Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, Matrix, Mattermost, Email, SMS, DingTalk, WeCom, Feishu, QQ, BlueBubbles, HomeAssistant.

modules.name: Plugin System
modules.description: Three discovery sources: ~/.hermes/plugins/, .hermes/plugins/, pip entry points. Two specialized plugin types: memory providers and context engines (single-select, one active at a time).

metadata.description: Any of 20 messaging platforms delivers normalized event to AIAgent Core
Core queries Provider Resolver for (provider, model) → (api_mode, key, base_url)
Core sends context; Prompt Builder assembles stable + context + volatile blocks
Load MEMORY.md/USER.md at session start; FTS5 search for historical context mid-session
Agent invokes tool; Registry resolves to appropriate handler from 70+ registered tools
Terminal tool routes to selected backend (Local/Docker/SSH/Daytona/Modal/Singularity)
After successful task: analyze path → save as SKILL.md → zero human curation
Extracted SKILL.md stored in filesystem; available for future sessions
When context exceeds threshold: lossy summarization of middle turns; preserves lineage
Plugin System loads user/project/pip plugins; injects custom tools and hooks into Core
Agent returns formatted response through originating channel adapter
Stable prompt blocks frozen at session start for Anthropic prefix caching optimization
If primary provider fails, Resolver triggers failover chain to next available provider

phases.name: Session Initialization
phases.description: Load identity, resolve provider, build initial prompt with cached stable blocks

steps.description: Channel adapter normalizes platform event into unified format
Load MEMORY.md (~800 tokens) + USER.md (~500 tokens); frozen snapshot for cache stability
Map (provider, model) tuple to (api_mode, key, base_url); check OAuth + credential pools
Tiered assembly: inject stable blocks into Anthropic cache breakpoints for prefix caching

phases.name: ReAct Execution Loop
phases.description: Core agent loop: Think → Act → Observe → Repeat. Skills auto-extracted from success paths.

steps.description: Model reasons about task; decides: respond directly or invoke tool(s)
If tool call: Registry matches tool name to registered handler with parameter validation
If terminal tool: route to selected backend (Docker/SSH/Daytona/Modal — serverless sleep/wake)
If context > threshold: lossy summarization of middle turns; preserve session lineage
Tool result feeds back into context; model decides: more tools or final answer
After task: analyze success path → auto-extract SKILL.md → store for future reuse

phases.name: Session Finalization
phases.description: Deliver response, persist memory summary, update FTS5 index

steps.description: Agent produces final text response; loop terminates
Response delivered through originating channel adapter to user
Key content auto-summarized; stored in SQLite FTS5 for future semantic search
